finding: useVirtualizer is element-based, but on the mobile native-scroll path getViewport() returns root:null so getScrollElement() yields null; the virtualizer never observes the window scroll, scrollOffset stays 0, and only the first overscan rows are ever rendered — scrolling a mobile conversation shows blank space / missing messages (a window virtualizer is required for that path).

scrollToMessageId starts an independent 3-frame rAF re-assert loop on every call with no cancellation of a prior in-flight loop; rapid find-next / successive jumps make the earlier match's loop keep re-scrolling to the previous index while the new loop targets the new one, so the view fights itself and jitters for ~50ms.

The initial bottom-jump (messagesEnd.scrollIntoView) runs while virtual rows are unmeasured, so it lands at the ESTIMATED total height (140px/row); once rows measure to their true heights the bottom shifts and nothing re-follows (measurement is not a messages change), so a conversation can open not pinned to the latest message with isAtBottom false.
The reassert rAF loop has no unmount/cancel guard; navigating away within ~50ms of a jump/find lets it call virt.scrollToIndex after the virtualizer's scroll element is torn down.

indexOfMessageId is added and unit-tested (TEST-1) with a doc comment claiming it 'Backs the virtualizer's scrollToMessageId (id → index → scrollToIndex)', but MessageList.tsx never imports/calls it — the imperative handle inlines arrRef.current.findIndex(m => m.id === id) in scrollToMessageId/captureAnchor/restoreAnchor. The test exercises dead code, not the real id→index path, so a regression in the actual lookup would go uncaught.

Virtualization unmounts off-screen rows, so a keyboard user who has focused an interactive control inside a message (link, code-copy button, chat-message-collapsible toggle) loses focus to <body> when that row scrolls out of the overscan window and unmounts; native Ctrl+F browser find and select-all-copy of the transcript also now only cover mounted rows — a regression from the prior full-render transcript.

The scrollToMessageId re-assert loop re-issues scrollToIndex on 3 successive animation frames (~50ms) with NO user-gesture cancellation, unlike the removed anchor-restore which aborted on wheel/touch/keydown; a user who scrolls immediately after a find/jump is yanked back to the target for the duration of the loop (brief scroll-jacking).
Reviewed (agent A): pendingAnchorRef lifecycle + observer-clear correctly handled; virt is a stable instance so the imperative-handle closures aren't stale — clean.
Reviewed (agent A): streaming-tail re-measure is inherent + single-row; getScrollElement identity churn is guarded by react-virtual's element-value comparison — clean.
Reviewed (agent B): all null/undefined edges guarded (getOffsetForIndex ?? [0], ?? 0, if(!msg)return null, getVirtualItemForOffset undefined check, null scroll element) — clean.

Reviewed (agent B): useVirtualizer setup (getScrollElement/measureElement/getItemKey/getTotalSize/absolute translateY) faithfully mirrors kit/table.tsx; forwardRef/useImperativeHandle idiomatic — clean.

Reviewed (agent C): MessageListHandle + scrollToMessage prop + optional getScrollElement/scrollerReady/virtualize types consistent with every call site; named export preserved; no other <MessageList caller; no desktop mirror — clean.

Reviewed (agent C): strictly safer — removes the querySelector(CSS.escape(id)) DOM lookups on the virtualized path, routes ids through pure findIndex→scrollToIndex; no dangerouslySetInnerHTML/unsafe DOM added — clean.
Reviewed (agent C): pure render change, no gating/permission code touched — clean.
Reviewed (agent C): no new user-facing strings/aria-labels; carried-over English literals consistent — clean.
